metadata.notes: Voice rewrite — conversational walkthrough style matching Wingspan gold standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Dice
- 2 standard six-sided dice — shared by all players, rolled once each turn for everyone

#### Realm Cards
- 11 realm cards per player set, each themed after a different Stonemaier Games title:
  - Between Two Castles, Between Two Cities, Charterstone, Euphoria, My Little Scythe, Pendulum, Scythe, Tapestry, The Society (Red Rising), Viticulture, Wingspan
- Each realm card is double-sided: standard side for learning, advanced side for experienced groups
- Dry-erase surface so you can play again and again

#### Player Components
- 1 resource card per player (dry-erase) — tracks your pumpkins, hearts, and coins
- 1 dry-erase marker per player

#### Other
- 1 round tracker card
- Rulebook with a realm-by-realm reference guide

**Checkpoint:** Each player should have their resource card, a marker, and the same 3 realm cards face-up in front of them (standard side for your first game). All resource tracks start at 0. Stars start at 0.

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Flows
- **3 rounds** — Each round, deal 3 realm cards from 11 available; everyone uses the same 3 realms
- **9 turns per round** — 27 turns total across the game
- **9 of 11 realms used** — 2 remain unused by game end

#### Round Setup
Deal 3 realm cards face-up. Every player places the same 3 realms in front of them.

#### What Happens Each Turn (Simultaneous)
1. **Roll dice**: One player rolls both dice. The result is public — everyone works with the same two numbers.
2. **Assign dice**: You independently assign one die to one realm and the other die to a different realm. You cannot send both dice to the same realm.
3. **Resolve effects**: Resolve each assigned die according to that realm's rules. This earns you stars (VP), resources, or special abilities.
4. **Track resources**: Update your resource card.

#### Your Three Resources

**Pumpkins — Adjust Existing Dice**
- Spend 2 pumpkins: Adjust one die by +1 or −1 (a 3 becomes a 2 or 4).
- Spend 3 pumpkins: Adjust one die by +/−1 AND reuse that die in the same realm where you already placed your other die this turn.
- Pumpkins tweak dice — they don't create new ones.

**Hearts — Copy a Rolled Value**
- Spend 2 hearts: If both dice show the same number (a pair), create a third die of that value.
- Spend 3 hearts: Create a third die equal to EITHER of the two rolled values (no pair required).
- Hearts always copy the original rolled values, not pumpkin-adjusted values.

**Coins — Create Any Die Value**
- Spend 2 coins: If the dice sum to exactly 7, create a third die equal to either rolled value.
- Spend X coins (1–6): Create a die showing X (e.g., spend 4 coins = a die showing 4).
- Most flexible resource, but also the most expensive.

#### Between Rounds
- **Leftover resources** — Worth 0.1 stars each (basically just a tiebreaker)
- **Resources reset to zero** — Do NOT carry over between rounds
- **New realms** — Discard the 3 realm cards and deal 3 fresh ones

#### Why Player Count Doesn't Matter
- **Simultaneous play** — Everyone uses the same dice and acts at the same time
- **No scaling** — A 6-player game takes exactly as long as a solo game

**Checkpoint:** Before your first turn, make sure everyone understands: one die per realm, two realms get dice each turn, one realm sits out. Resources reset between rounds — spend them or lose them.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Dice Assignment Rules
- Each turn you assign exactly 1 die to one realm and the other die to a different realm. Your third realm gets nothing that turn.
- You choose which die goes where — this is your main decision every turn.
- A realm can receive a die on multiple turns within the same round — it's not locked out after one use.
- Each realm can generally only be activated once per turn (1 die), unless you spend 3 pumpkins to reuse that realm.
- If you can't or don't want to use a die, it goes in the crate at its rolled value. You get 1 compensatory resource per unused die.

#### Resource Rules
- Pumpkins adjust dice. Hearts copy rolled values. Coins create any value.
- Resources always reference the **rolled** values, not adjusted ones. If you pumpkin-adjust a 4 to a 5, the dice are still considered 4 and 5 (not a pair) for hearts.
- Resources reset to zero between rounds. Leftover resources at round end = 0.1 stars each.

#### Realm Highlights
- **Viticulture** — Row of grape vine values (1–6); mark off numbers to complete contiguous sequences; longer chains score big
- **Wingspan** — Grid of bird nest eggs; fill nests matching die values; completing rows or columns earns bonus stars
- **Scythe** — Small hex grid; claim hexes matching die numbers; adjacent claimed hexes form regions that score exponentially
- **Charterstone** — Building track (1–6); build at a position to generate ongoing bonuses for the rest of the round; invest early
- **Between Two Castles** — Fill squares where each number must be lower than the one below it; your score is LIMITED to the score of your LOWEST realm that round
- **Between Two Cities** — Write numbers in a 3x3 grid; no identical numbers orthogonally adjacent; scores as the lower of your other two realms' scores
- **The Society (Red Rising)** — Numbers must be placed in strictly ascending order; no duplicates allowed

#### Advanced Realm Sides
- **Flip any realm card** — Advanced side offers extra scoring conditions, resource interactions, and combo potential
- **Player agreement** — Use all standard, all advanced, or any mix

#### Solo Mode
- **Same rules** — Chase target scores instead of competing
- **Targets** — Peasant (25+), Citizen (35+), Lord (45+), Ruler (55+)

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- The game ends after the 9th turn of Round 3 (27th turn total)

#### Scoring
- **Stars are your only currency** — Cumulative star total across all 3 rounds is your final score
1. **Realm scoring** — Stars earned by assigning dice to realms and meeting their conditions.
2. **Pattern bonuses** — Some realms grant bonus stars for completed patterns (rows, sequences, sets, regions).
3. **Unused resources** — 0.1 stars each (tiebreaker value only).

#### Winning
- **Most stars wins** — Tie: most remaining resources (summed across all types); still tied: shared victory

#### Typical Scores
- First-time players: 20–30 stars
- Experienced players: 35–50 stars
- Expert play with advanced realms: 45–60+ stars

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Read All 3 Realms Before Turn 1
- **Study before rolling** — Learn what each realm rewards: high numbers, low numbers, specific values, sequences, or spatial patterns
- **Assign intelligently** — Knowing realm needs lets you make good choices from the very first roll

#### Spend Resources Aggressively
- **Unused resources are nearly worthless** — Only 0.1 stars each at round end
- **Convert mediocre rolls** — Spend pumpkins and hearts to make bad dice productive
- **Don't hoard** — Waiting for "the perfect moment" usually means losing resources at round end

#### 3 Pumpkins for Realm Reuse Is Powerful
- **Double activation** — Spending 3 pumpkins lets you activate one realm twice in a single turn
- **Prioritize pumpkin generation** — This is a massive advantage whenever the opportunity appears

#### Prioritize Compounding Realms
- **Charterstone** — Ongoing bonuses apply to future turns; assign a die on Turn 1 for 8 turns of payoff
- **One-shot realms can wait** — Scythe hexes and similar score the same whether placed early or late

#### Rotate Which Realm Sits Out
- **One realm skips each turn** — Rotate the idle realm so each gets at least 2–3 dice per round
- **Zero dice = zero score** — A realm that never gets a die is a complete waste of a card slot

#### High Dice Aren't Always Better
- **Patterns over raw numbers** — Many realms reward specific values, not the highest
- **Example** — A 2 might be worth more than a 6 in Viticulture if it completes a long contiguous sequence

#### Watch Realm Interactions
- **Between Two Castles** — Caps your score at your lowest realm, so you must keep all 3 productive
- **Between Two Cities** — Scores as the lower of your other two realms
- **Together** — These force you to think holistically and balance all realms

#### Common Mistakes
- Assigning dice by highest value without checking what each realm actually needs.
- Forgetting to use hearts and pumpkins — they expire at round end with near-zero value.
- Thinking resources carry across rounds (they reset to 0 each time!).
- Feeding one realm all the dice while another gets nothing.
- Not reading advanced sides when playing with experienced groups.
- Rushing turns without considering which realm benefits most from sitting out.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
- Deal 3 realm cards face-up — everyone uses the same 3
- Take your resource card and marker
- Everything starts at 0: pumpkins, hearts, coins, and stars

#### Round 1, Turn 1
- **Roll both dice** — Say you get a 3 and a 5
- **Read your 3 realms** — Which one wants a 3? Which benefits most from a 5?
- **Assign 1 die to each of 2 realms** — The third realm sits out this turn
- **Resolve effects** — Mark any stars earned or resources gained

#### Round 1, Turns 2–3
- **Form a plan** — Which realm needs multiple dice to complete a pattern? Which scores well with just 1?
- **Use pumpkins** — Adjust a die by +/-1 when you're close to completing something valuable

#### Round 1, Turns 4–9
- **Spend hearts** — Create a third die when possible for a bonus realm activation
- **Spend coins** — Use on hard-to-reach values
- **Don't hoard** — Resources reset at round end

#### End of Round 1
- **Tally stars** — From all 3 realms
- **Unused resources** — Worth 0.1 stars each (barely anything)
- **Reset** — Resources go to 0; discard the 3 realm cards

#### Rounds 2–3
- **Deal 3 new realms** — Study them and adapt assignments to each round's unique combination
- **Stars accumulate** — Your total carries across all 3 rounds

#### Final Score
- Add up stars from all 3 rounds
- **First game target** — 25–35 stars is solid; 40+ is excellent

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Realm Synergy Reading
- **Identify producers and consumers** — When 3 realms are dealt, spot which generates resources and which consumes them
- **Common producers** — Charterstone and Euphoria tend to produce pumpkins
- **Common consumers** — Wingspan and Viticulture reward precise values that pumpkins help hit
- **Chain them** — Feed your resource producer into your resource consumer

#### Dice Allocation Math
- **Obvious play isn't always optimal** — A 3 on Charterstone building a bonus that pays out for 8 more turns can beat a 6 on Scythe for immediate points
- **Calculate compound value** — Not just the face value of each placement

#### Resource Spending Thresholds
- **Pumpkins (2)** — Spend when adjusting +/-1 completes a pattern
- **Pumpkins (3)** — Spend when reusing a realm would earn 3+ stars
- **Hearts (3)** — Spend freely whenever a third die would score in a realm
- **Hearts (2)** — Spend only when pairs happen naturally
- **Coins (X for value X)** — Best when you need a specific number that pumpkins can't reach (typically a 1 or 6)

#### The Between Two Problem
- **Triple constraint** — When Castles and Cities appear together, Castles caps you at your lowest realm, and Cities scores as the lower of the other two
- **Solution** — Keep all 3 realms within 2–3 stars of each other; never let one run away while another languishes

#### Advanced Side Combos
- **Resource chains** — Some advanced sides generate resources that others consume
- **Example** — Pendulum's advanced side produces coins on specific values; Tapestry's advanced side spends coins for bonus stars
- **Lean in hard** — When these appear together, commit to the combo

#### Turn 1 Priority Order
1. **Charterstone** — Ongoing bonus compounds over 8 remaining turns.
2. **Pumpkin/heart producers** — Any realm producing resources on first activation.
3. **High-value single placements** — Scythe central hex, Viticulture middle sequence.
4. **Everything else** — Score what you can.

rulings: Pumpkins adjust existing dice — they do NOT create new dice. Spend 2 to adjust +/-1; spend 3 to adjust AND reuse in the same realm.
Hearts and coins reference the ROLLED values, not adjusted values. A die adjusted from 4 to 5 is still considered a 4 for hearts/coins purposes.
Unused dice go to the crate at their ROLLED value (not adjusted). You receive 1 compensatory resource per unused die.
Resources reset to zero between rounds. Unused resources are worth 0.1 stars each at round end.
Each realm can generally only be activated once per turn (1 die), unless a 3-pumpkin spend allows reuse in the same realm.
In The Society (Red Rising) realm, numbers must be placed in strictly ascending order. No duplicates allowed.
Between Two Castles realm caps your score at your lowest realm that round. Between Two Cities scores as the lower of your other two realms.
